title: Kidangoor Subramanya Swamy Temple
tagline: Kidangoor Subramanya Swamy Temple — spiritual town of Kerala

overview.short: Kidangoor Subramanya Swami Temple is an ancient Hindu temple located in Kidangoor near Ayarkkunnam in Kottayam district in the Indian state of Kerala.
overview.description: Kidangoor Subramanya Swami Temple is an ancient Hindu temple located in Kidangoor near Ayarkkunnam in Kottayam district in the Indian state of Kerala. It is one of the renowned Subramanya temples in Kerala which is estimated to be at least 1500 years old.

routes.byAir: Fly to nearest airport + road transfer
Fly to nearest airport + road transfer

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Kidangoor, Kottayam
topPlaces.description: Kidangoor is a village in Kottayam district, in Kerala. It is located between the two major towns in Kottayam District, Kottayam and Pala.

topPlaces.name: Amayannoor
topPlaces.description: Amayannoor is a village in Kottayam district in the Indian state of Kerala. Administratively, it falls under Ayarkkunnam panchayath.

topPlaces.name: Kezhuvamkulam
topPlaces.description: Kezhuvamkulam is a village in the Kottayam District in the Indian state of Kerala.

topPlaces.name: Kadaplamattom
topPlaces.description: Kadaplamattom is a small village near Palai, Kottayam District, Kerala, India. It is a largely agrarian community, with most of the population having agricultural roles within it.

topPlaces.name: Cherpunkal
topPlaces.description: Cherpunkal is a small town in the Kottayam district of Kerala State in India on the Kottayam-Palai Road, seven kilometers from Pala city. Cherpunkal is famous for Mar Sleeva Forane Catholic Church, believed to have been established by Thomas the Apostle.

topPlaces.name: Puliyannoor Mahadeva Temple
topPlaces.description: Puliyannoor Mahadeva Temple is a Hindu temple. It is located in Puliyannoor in Mutholy panchayath and near Pala in Kottayam district in the Indian state of Kerala.

topPlaces.name: Koodalloor
topPlaces.description: Koodalloor is a village in Kottayam district, Kerala, India. It is strategically located 7 Kilometers off from Ettumanoor - Pala Road (deviated at Koodalloor Kavala Jn), 2 km from Kadaplamttom 24 km from Kottayam town.

topPlaces.name: Kadappoor
topPlaces.description: Kadappooru is a village in Kottayam district, Kerala, India. It is located 3 kilometers from Ettumanoor - Pala Road (deviated at Koodalloor Kavala Jn), 5 km off from Cochin - Kottayam Road (deviated at Vempally Jn) and 18 km from Kottayam town.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Kidangoor Subramanya Swamy Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Kidangoor Subramanya Swamy Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Kidangoor Subramanya Swamy Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Cochin International Airport, Kochi (58 km) and the nearest railway station is Ernakulam Junction (Kochi) (48 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Kidangoor Subramanya Swamy Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Kidangoor Subramanya Swamy Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Kidangoor Subramanya Swamy Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Kidangoor Subramanya Swamy Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Kidangoor Subramanya Swamy Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Kidangoor, Kottayam, Amayannoor, Kezhuvamkulam, Kadaplamattom, Cherpunkal.
